text: No communication made in good faith is an offence by reason of any harm to the person to whom it is made, if it is made for the benefit of that person.
section_title: No communication made in good faith is an offence by reason of any harm to the person to whom it is made, if it is made for the benefit of that person.

text: The main rule states that a communication made in good faith and intended for the benefit of the recipient does not constitute an offence even if it results in harm to the person receiving the communication.
section_title: No communication made in good faith is an offence by reason of any harm to the person to whom it is made, if it is made for the benefit of that person.

text: A, a surgeon, communicates his opinion that a patient cannot live in good faith. The patient dies due to the shock caused by this information. Despite knowing it might cause death, A has committed no offence.
section_title: No communication made in good faith is an offence by reason of any harm to the person to whom it is made, if it is made for the benefit of that person.
